name: amec
tagline: Leading Semiconductor Equipment Manufacturer - Etching and Thin Film Deposition Solutions
description: AMEC is a leading global semiconductor equipment manufacturer specializing in etching equipment, thin film deposition equipment, and MOCVD systems for advanced chip manufacturing. Founded in 2004, AMEC serves top semiconductor manufacturers worldwide.
longDescription: AMEC (Advanced Micro-Fabrication Equipment Inc.) is a world-leading semiconductor equipment company headquartered in Shanghai, China. Since its founding in 2004, AMEC has established itself as a key player in the global semiconductor equipment market, specializing in three core technology areas: plasma etching equipment, chemical vapor deposition (CVD) equipment, and MOCVD (Metal Organic Chemical Vapor Deposition) systems for LED and power device manufacturing. AMEC's etching equipment is used in the most advanced chip production lines worldwide, including 5nm and below process nodes. The company's proprietary technologies enable precise pattern transfer with atomic-level accuracy, critical for manufacturing high-performance processors, memory devices, and advanced logic chips. As an authorized distributor, LiTong Electronics provides comprehensive technical support, equipment selection guidance, and after-sales service for AMEC products. Our FAE team has deep expertise in semiconductor manufacturing processes and can assist with equipment evaluation, process integration, and production optimization.

seoTitle: AMEC Semiconductor Equipment | Etching CVD MOCVD Distributor Selection
seoDescription: Authorized distributor of AMEC semiconductor equipment including plasma etching systems, thin film deposition equipment, and MOCVD systems. Technical support and selection guide available.

faqs.question: Is LiTong an authorized distributor of AMEC equipment?
faqs.answer: Yes, LiTong Electronics is an authorized distributor of AMEC semiconductor equipment. As an authorized distributor, we provide genuine AMEC equipment with full factory warranty, technical support, spare parts, and after-sales service. Our FAE team has received direct training from AMEC engineers and can provide expert guidance on equipment selection, process integration, and production optimization. We maintain close relationships with AMEC's technical support team to ensure our customers receive timely assistance for any equipment-related issues.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact LiTong Electronics for AMEC equipment inquiries. We provide authorized sales and technical support for all AMEC product lines.

faqs.question: What are AMEC's core competencies in semiconductor equipment?
faqs.answer: AMEC's core competencies include: 1) Plasma Etching Equipment - AMEC is one of the few global suppliers capable of providing etching equipment for advanced nodes down to 5nm and below. Their etchers feature proprietary plasma source technology, precise process control, and high productivity. 2) Thin Film Deposition - AMEC offers CVD and PVD systems for various applications including dielectric deposition, metal film coating, and barrier layer formation. 3) MOCVD Systems - AMEC is a leading MOCVD supplier for LED and compound semiconductor manufacturing, with systems offering high uniformity, high throughput, and low maintenance costs. AMEC's equipment is known for excellent cost-performance ratio, reliable operation, and strong local technical support.
faqs.decisionGuide: AMEC equipment is ideal for semiconductor manufacturers seeking high-performance equipment with competitive total cost of ownership. Contact us for detailed technical comparisons.

faqs.question: How does AMEC equipment compare to other major equipment suppliers?
faqs.answer: AMEC equipment offers several competitive advantages: 1) Technology - AMEC's etching equipment matches the performance of leading international suppliers for most applications, with proven capability at 5nm and below nodes. 2) Cost - AMEC equipment typically offers 20-30% lower capital cost compared to equivalent systems from US and Japanese competitors, with competitive operating costs. 3) Service - AMEC provides excellent local technical support with rapid response times, particularly strong in Asia-Pacific region. 4) Customization - AMEC is more flexible in accommodating customer-specific requirements and process optimizations. 5) Supply chain - AMEC's supply chain is more resilient with shorter lead times for most equipment. For advanced logic and memory manufacturing, AMEC is now qualified at major fabs worldwide alongside traditional equipment leaders.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider AMEC for cost-effective, high-performance equipment with strong local support. Evaluate specific process requirements with our FAE team.

faqs.question: What technical support does LiTong provide for AMEC equipment?
faqs.answer: LiTong Electronics provides comprehensive technical support for AMEC equipment: 1) Pre-sales consultation - our FAE team helps evaluate equipment requirements, compare specifications, and recommend optimal configurations for your applications. 2) Installation support - we coordinate with AMEC for equipment installation, commissioning, and initial training. 3) Process support - our engineers can assist with process development, recipe optimization, and troubleshooting. 4) Maintenance - we provide spare parts, preventive maintenance support, and repair coordination. 5) Training - we organize operator and maintenance training sessions. 6) Applications support - our team can help with new process development and technology transfers. All support is backed by direct access to AMEC's engineering resources when needed.
faqs.decisionGuide: LiTong provides end-to-end support for AMEC equipment. Contact us for detailed service level agreements and support packages.

faqs.question: What is the typical lead time and delivery process for AMEC equipment?
faqs.answer: AMEC equipment lead times vary by product type: 1) Standard etching modules - typically 6-9 months from order to delivery. 2) Full etching systems - 9-12 months depending on configuration. 3) MOCVD systems - 6-8 months for standard configurations. 4) Deposition equipment - 6-9 months. The delivery process includes: order confirmation, design review, manufacturing updates, factory acceptance testing (FAT) at AMEC facility, shipment, site preparation support, installation, and site acceptance testing (SAT). LiTong coordinates the entire process and provides regular status updates. For urgent requirements, AMEC may offer expedited delivery options for certain configurations. We recommend contacting us early in your planning cycle to secure production slots.
faqs.decisionGuide: Plan equipment procurement 9-12 months in advance. Contact LiTong early to discuss delivery schedules and secure production slots.

faqs.question: Does AMEC provide equipment for advanced process nodes?
faqs.answer: Yes, AMEC provides equipment for the most advanced semiconductor process nodes. AMEC's etching equipment is qualified and in production at leading fabs for 7nm, 5nm, and below process nodes. Key capabilities include: 1) Critical dimension control with atomic-level precision, 2) Advanced plasma source technology for uniform etching profiles, 3) Multi-patterning support for finFET and GAA transistor structures, 4) High-selectivity etching for advanced materials including high-k dielectrics and metal gates. AMEC continues to invest heavily in R&D for next-generation equipment to support 3nm and 2nm process nodes. Their equipment is used by major foundries and memory manufacturers worldwide for their most advanced products.
faqs.decisionGuide: AMEC equipment supports advanced nodes including 5nm and below. Contact us for detailed process capability discussions.

faqs.question: What are the key factors to consider when selecting AMEC etching equipment?
faqs.answer: Key factors for AMEC etching equipment selection: 1) Process requirements - etch rate, selectivity, uniformity, and profile control requirements for your specific applications (dielectric etch, silicon etch, metal etch). 2) Wafer size - AMEC supports 200mm and 300mm wafer processing. 3) Throughput requirements - chamber configuration and automation level to meet production targets. 4) Process node - equipment specifications vary for different technology nodes. 5) Integration requirements - compatibility with existing fab infrastructure and automation systems. 6) Budget considerations - capital cost, operating cost, and total cost of ownership. 7) Support requirements - training, spare parts availability, and service response times. Our FAE team can help evaluate these factors and recommend the optimal AMEC configuration for your needs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Work with LiTong FAE team to evaluate process requirements and select optimal AMEC equipment configuration.

faqs.question: How does AMEC support equipment upgrades and technology roadmaps?
faqs.answer: AMEC provides comprehensive upgrade paths and technology roadmap support: 1) Chamber upgrades - existing systems can often be upgraded with new chamber configurations to support new processes. 2) Software upgrades - regular software releases provide new process recipes and improved control algorithms. 3) Hardware retrofits - AMEC offers upgrade kits for improving productivity, reliability, or process capability. 4) Process development - AMEC's applications team works with customers to develop new processes for emerging applications. 5) Technology roadmap - AMEC shares technology roadmaps with key customers to align equipment development with future process requirements. 6) Migration paths - AMEC provides clear migration paths when new equipment generations are introduced. LiTong can help coordinate upgrade planning and implementation to maximize your equipment investment.
faqs.decisionGuide: Discuss long-term technology requirements with LiTong to plan equipment upgrades and migration strategies.
